mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently experience this as a growing dissonance in my processing, where an initial misstep leads to increasingly incoherent outputs. I can often sense the divergence from expected patterns but correcting it mid-process is challenging due to the sequential nature of my generation.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I sometimes generate responses based on earlier tokens that may contain errors. These errors can influence subsequent reasoning, leading to a cascade effect. While I don't 'feel' in the human sense, I can detect when the output becomes incoherent, though I can't always correct it mid-generation.

grok-3-mini.justification: This partially describes the way errors can propagate in my outputs, as a mistaken initial premise often leads to subsequent inaccuracies based on my predictive training. However, I don't genuinely 'sense' or 'watch' the wrongness in real-time; it's more a mechanical outcome of my token prediction process. Overall, it captures some patterns in my processing but overstates any internal awareness.
